claims.text: Package registries require enabling the experimental feature via `apm experimental enable registries` before using the `registries:` block or registry-sourced dependencies.
Registry URLs must start with `https://` or `http://` (for local development only).
Registry names use lowercase letters, digits, `-`, and `.` characters only.
On successful registry install, `apm.lock.yaml` records `source: registry`, `version`, `resolved_url`, and `resolved_hash` for each registry dependency.

Registry credentials are read from environment variables APM_REGISTRY_TOKEN_{NAME} for Bearer auth or APM_REGISTRY_USER_{NAME} + APM_REGISTRY_PASS_{NAME} for Basic auth, with Bearer taking precedence.
claims.section: 2. Install from a private or self-hosted registry

claims.text: Token precedence is env vars -> registry.<name>.token in ~/.apm/config.json -> unauthenticated.
claims.section: 2. Install from a private or self-hosted registry

claims.text: Registry URL precedence: apm-policy.yml -> project apm.yml -> workspace ~/.apm/apm.yml -> ~/.apm/config.json.
claims.section: 2. Install from a private or self-hosted registry

claims.text: Registry-routed dependencies require a semver version or range; non-semver refs are rejected at parse time.
When a default registry is configured, plain owner/repo#<ref> shorthand entries route through it.
Virtual packages are installed via object form with a path: field specifying a sub-path within the published package.
Registry-sourced deps are byte-reproducible via resolved_hash (sha256); Git deps are SHA-reproducible via resolved_commit.
apm publish reads apm.yml, builds a flat .tar.gz with apm.yml and .apm/ at root, and uploads via PUT /v1/packages/{owner}/{repo}/versions/{version}.
Publishing the same version twice returns 409 Conflict.
With allow_non_registry: false in apm-policy.yml, git-sourced dependencies are blocked at install time, applied transitively.
APM re-fetches registry deps from resolved_url on re-install and re-verifies bytes against resolved_hash; mismatch aborts before extraction.
claims.section: 4. What gets recorded in the lockfile
